page.title: Marbella Jewellery Week 2026 Yacht Charter Guide

data.meta_description: Navigate Marbella Jewellery Week 2026 in style with a private yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Browse our fleet, compare prices, and book your day on the water.
data.summary: A practical guide to attending Marbella Jewellery Week 2026 by yacht, covering logistics, fleet options, and how to combine the event with a day on the Costa del Sol.
key_facts: Marbella Jewellery Week 2026 runs in early summer; exact dates confirmed by organisers.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, a 5-minute walk from the jewellery fair venue.
Our fleet includes 18 boats from €749/2h to €4,719 minimum 4h for a Mangusta 80.
Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.
The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) comes with a free jet ski on 4h+ bookings.
For groups of 5–12, Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) offer the best value.
Book via WhatsApp for pricing on larger vessels like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86.

faq.a: Marbella Jewellery Week 2026 is scheduled for early summer, typically late May or early June. Exact dates are confirmed by the organisers closer to the event. Check the official MJW website for the latest schedule. All our yachts depart from Puerto Banús, which is adjacent to the fair venues.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht for just a few hours to attend the jewellery fair?
faq.a: Yes. Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. That gives you enough time to cruise from Puerto Banús to the fair, enjoy a short trip, and return. For a full day, 8-hour charters are available from €2,299.

faq.q: Which boat is best for a group of 10 to 12 during Jewellery Week?
faq.a: For groups of 10–12, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is our most popular choice. It departs from Puerto Banús, includes a free jet ski on 4h+ bookings, and costs €4,719 minimum 4 hours. Alternatively, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a spacious flybridge for socialising.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). No hidden fees. You just need to bring sunscreen and your swimwear.

faq.q: Do I need to book in advance for Jewellery Week?
faq.a: Yes, we strongly recommend booking at least 2–3 weeks ahead. Jewellery Week attracts a large crowd, and our fleet books up quickly. Contact us via WhatsApp to check availability for your preferred date and boat size.

faq.q: Can I combine the jewellery fair with a trip to Cabopino or Estepona?
faq.a: Absolutely. Most charters allow you to set your own itinerary. A 4-hour trip can cover Puerto Banús to Cabopino (3 NM east) and back, with a swim stop at Cala del Faro. An 8-hour charter can reach Estepona (12 NM west) or even Sotogrande (20 NM west) for lunch.

Cancellations made 7 days or more before departure receive a full refund. Between 3 and 7 days, a 50% refund applies. Less than 3 days, no refund. We recommend travel insurance for any unforeseen changes. Contact us directly to discuss your booking.

faq.q: Is there parking near the marina for Jewellery Week?
faq.a: Puerto Banús has several paid parking garages within a 5-minute walk of the marina. During Jewellery Week, spaces fill quickly. We suggest arriving early or using a taxi/ride-share. Alternatively, charter a yacht and arrive by sea – no traffic, no parking hassle.

data.body_html:
Marbella Jewellery Week 2026 (MJW) is the Costa del Sol's premier showcase for fine jewellery, watches, and luxury accessories. Held in the heart of Puerto Banús, the event attracts collectors, designers, and buyers from across Europe. If you're planning to attend, combining the fair with a private yacht charter is the most stylish way to navigate the week. This guide covers everything you need to know: boat options, pricing, logistics, and how to make the most of your day on the water.

Why charter a yacht for Marbella Jewellery Week?

Puerto Banús is built around its marina. The jewellery fair venues are within a 5-minute walk from our departure pontoons. Chartering a yacht means you can anchor steps from the action, avoid traffic jams along the Golden Mile, and enjoy a private space for pre- or post-fair drinks. Plus, the afternoon sun on the Mediterranean is a welcome break from the showroom lights.

Our fleet: from speedboats to superyachts

We operate 18 vessels from Puerto Banús, ranging from a 6.4m speedboat (5 pax) to a 29m Ferretti 94 (12 pax). For Marbella Jewellery Week, the most popular choices are:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) – €749 for 2 hours, €2,299 for 8 hours. Ideal for couples or small groups.
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – same pricing, slightly more space. Great for a family outing.
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – €4,719 minimum 4 hours, includes a free jet ski. The head-turner of the fleet.
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) – spacious flybridge, perfect for entertaining.

For larger groups (10–12 pax), the Mangusta 80 (white or grey) or the Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax) offer the best combination of luxury and capacity. Contact us via WhatsApp for pricing on these and other boats.

Pricing and what's included

All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras. Here are the key price points for Jewellery Week:

BoatCapacity2 hours4 hours8 hours
Astondoa 40 / Azimut 399–11 pax€749On request€2,299
Mangusta 80 (all colours)12 pax–€4,719 (incl. jet ski)On request
Other boats (Ferretti 94, Canados 86, etc.)12 paxPricing on request via WhatsApp

Sample itinerary: Jewellery Week by yacht

A typical day might look like this:

  • 10:00 – Board your yacht at Puerto Banús. Meet your skipper, stow bags, enjoy welcome drinks.
  • 10:30 – Cruise east past the Golden Mile and Marbella Marina. Spot La Concha mountain in the background.
  • 11:00 – Anchor at Cala del Faro for a swim. The water is typically 22–25°C in June.
  • 12:30 – Return to Puerto Banús. Disembark and walk to the jewellery fair.
  • 16:00 – Reboard for an afternoon cruise west to Cabopino or Estepona. Snacks and drinks on board.
  • 19:00 – Sunset return to Puerto Banús, just in time for evening events.

This works perfectly with a 4-hour morning charter and a 4-hour afternoon charter, or a single 8-hour trip. Adjust as you like – your skipper is flexible.

Booking tips for Jewellery Week 2026

Jewellery Week is one of the busiest periods in Puerto Banús. Here's how to secure your yacht:

  • Book at least 3 weeks in advance – our fleet often sells out.
  • For groups of 10+, consider the Mangusta 80 or Azimut 58 Flybridge.
  • If you're on a tighter budget, the Bandido (11.5m, 8 pax) or Red Tide (11m, 6 pax) are excellent value – pricing on request.
  • All bookings are made via WhatsApp. We'll confirm availability, send a payment link, and answer any questions.

For more details on all our boats, visit our Marbella yacht charter hub.

Where to eat and drink near the fair

Puerto Banús has dozens of restaurants within walking distance of the marina. For a post-fair lunch, try Los Bandidos for fresh seafood or Bibo Marbella for Dani García's tapas. For a sundowner, the rooftop at Ocean Club is a classic. If you're on a charter, your skipper can recommend spots accessible by water taxi.

Weather and sea conditions in June

June in Marbella averages 28°C air temperature and 22°C sea temperature. Winds are typically light (5–10 knots from the east), making for calm seas. The sun sets around 21:30, giving you long evenings on the water. Bring a light jacket for the breeze after sunset.

Frequently asked questions

When is Marbella Jewellery Week 2026?

Marbella Jewellery Week 2026 is scheduled for early summer, typically late May or early June. Exact dates are confirmed by the organisers closer to the event. Check the official MJW website for the latest schedule. All our yachts depart from Puerto Banús, which is adjacent to the fair venues.

Can I charter a yacht for just a few hours to attend the jewellery fair?

Yes. Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. That gives you enough time to cruise from Puerto Banús to the fair, enjoy a short trip, and return. For a full day, 8-hour charters are available from €2,299.

Which boat is best for a group of 10 to 12 during Jewellery Week?

For groups of 10–12, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is our most popular choice. It departs from Puerto Banús, includes a free jet ski on 4h+ bookings, and costs €4,719 minimum 4 hours. Alternatively, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a spacious flybridge for socialising.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). No hidden fees. You just need to bring sunscreen and your swimwear.

Do I need to book in advance for Jewellery Week?

Yes, we strongly recommend booking at least 2–3 weeks ahead. Jewellery Week attracts a large crowd, and our fleet books up quickly. Contact us via WhatsApp to check availability for your preferred date and boat size.

Can I combine the jewellery fair with a trip to Cabopino or Estepona?

Absolutely. Most charters allow you to set your own itinerary. A 4-hour trip can cover Puerto Banús to Cabopino (3 NM east) and back, with a swim stop at Cala del Faro. An 8-hour charter can reach Estepona (12 NM west) or even Sotogrande (20 NM west) for lunch.

What is the cancellation policy?

Cancellations made 7 days or more before departure receive a full refund. Between 3 and 7 days, a 50% refund applies. Less than 3 days, no refund. We recommend travel insurance for any unforeseen changes. Contact us directly to discuss your booking.

Is there parking near the marina for Jewellery Week?

Puerto Banús has several paid parking garages within a 5-minute walk of the marina. During Jewellery Week, spaces fill quickly. We suggest arriving early or using a taxi/ride-share. Alternatively, charter a yacht and arrive by sea – no traffic, no parking hassle.
